questions.text: Which of the following best describes the purpose of React's useState hook?

options.text: To manage side effects in a component
To manage local component state across renders
To handle API requests
To optimize component performance

questions.text: What is the primary benefit of using a custom React hook?

options.text: It improves rendering performance automatically
It allows reuse of stateful logic across components
It replaces the need for components
It eliminates the need for props

questions.text: In a React application, why is it recommended to keep components small and focused?

options.text: To reduce bundle size automatically
To make state management unnecessary
To improve readability, reusability, and maintainability
To avoid using hooks

questions.text: What problem does an abstraction layer (like a separate API module) solve in frontend applications?

options.text: It eliminates the need for backend services
It separates data fetching logic from UI components
It makes the UI render faster automatically
It removes the need for state management

questions.text: Why is it important to disable navigation (e.g., Next button) until an answer is selected?

options.text: To prevent unnecessary re-renders
To enforce complete user input and avoid invalid states
To reduce API calls
To improve styling consistency

questions.text: What is the main advantage of persisting quiz progress in localStorage?

options.text: It improves rendering performance
It allows the application to work offline only
It enables users to resume progress after a page refresh
It replaces backend storage permanently
